named_technique: DP-coloring + Bernshteyn-Kostochka-Pron DP-critical density + DP-edge-deletion rigidity (Dvorak-Postle 2018)
bridge_lemma: For hypothetical 4-vertex-critical no-critical-edge G, every G-e contains an inclusion-minimal DP-4-critical core H_e: every edge drops chi_DP to 3 (Bernshteyn 2017 rigidity), and BKP DP-critical density forces 2|E(H_e)| >= (40/13)|V(H_e)| unless H_e = K_4. Every edge of G is thus edge-avoidable inside a dense DP-4-critical core. Open: does 4-vertex-criticality force chi_DP(G-v) <= 3, making each H_e spanning and giving 2|E(G)| >= (40/13)|V(G)|+2?
informal_proof_outline: Goal. Resolve Dirac k = 4: exhibit, or prove the non-existence of, a finite 4-vertex-critical graph G with chromatic number 4 such that no single-edge deletion drops chromatic. Background. Prior submissions on this gap: bare-lane (slot 1271), Skottova-Steiner fusion (slot 1295), discharging (W2), and spectral HNTW (slot 1303). DP-coloring transplant. The DP-chromatic number chi_DP(G) is the smallest k such that every k-fold cover of G admits an independent transversal (Dvorak-Postle 2018, J Combin Theory B). DP-coloring dominates list-coloring which dominates ordinary coloring, so chi(G) <= chi_l(G) <= chi_DP(G). For our G: chi(G) = 4 immediately gives chi_DP(G) >= 4. For every edge e in E(G), chi(G - e) = 4 forces chi_DP(G - e) >= 4 as well; pick an inclusion-minimal H_e subseteq G - e with chi_DP(H_e) >= 4 and chi_DP(H_e - f) <= 3 for every edge f of H_e. This is the DP-4-critical core associated to e. By the Bernshteyn-Kostochka-Pron 2017 DP-critical density theorem, H_e != K_4 implies 2 |E(H_e)| >= (40/13) |V(H_e)|, which is strictly stronger than the Dirac chi-critical density (2 |E| >= (5/2) n - 1 for ordinary 4-critical graphs) and matches the Borodin-Kostochka conjectured threshold for square-free 4-chromatic graphs. The DP-edge-deletion rigidity result of Bernshteyn 2017 ('DP-colorings are more rigid than list-colorings under edge removal') states that for any DP-critical graph H, removing any edge strictly drops chi_DP (i.e. the DP analogue of every-edge-critical is automatic for inclusion-minimal cores). Strategy outline. Step 1: package the DP-coloring framework into Lean predicates and inequalities (lemma candidates 1-3 below). Step 2: extract from each G - e its inclusion-minimal DP-4-critical subgraph H_e and apply the BKP density bound (lemma 4). Step 3: identify the edge-avoidable DP-core obstruction: every edge of G is missing from at least one dense DP-4-critical core; this is a clean combinatorial-extremal restriction. Step 4: the strong branch. Assume (and audit-flag as an honest hypothesis) that ordinary 4-vertex-criticality of G forces chi_DP(G - v) <= 3 for all v (i.e. G is also DP-vertex-critical); then every H_e is spanning and we obtain the global double-count 2 |E(G)| - 2 >= (40/13) |V(G)|, hence the edge-density bound |E(G)| >= (20/13) |V(G)| + 1, which is technique-disjoint from the spectral (HNTW) and Skottova-Steiner bounds. Step 5: handoff. Aristotle's MCGS attempts either (a) a non-existence argument via the density restriction + Hajos-calculus reachability on small dense DP-4-critical graphs, or (b) an explicit DP-edge-avoidable witness. Honest assessment. The DP-vertex-critical strengthening hypothesis is the dangerous link; without it the global bound becomes per-edge rather than spanning, weaker. Aristotle is invited either to prove the strengthening or to construct a DP-critical witness directly. Audit gate. mathematical_content_score target >= 4 if the DP density bound is formalized at k = 4; <= 2 if Aristotle merely restates the gap. Falsification path. A DP-vertex-critical witness for k = 4 (if it exists in Lean's libraries or via Hajos-calculus reachability) immediately yields the ordinary witness via the chi <= chi_DP chain (no, since equality is not forced).
candidate_lemmas: ordinary_chromatic_le_dp_chromatic: For every finite simple graph G, the ordinary chromatic number chi(G) <= chi_DP(G), the DP-chromatic number, where chi_DP(G) is defined as the least k such that every k-fold cover of G admits an independent transversal (Dvorak-Postle 2018 JCTB Definition 2.1).

no_critical_edge_gives_dp_core: If G satisfies chi(G - e) = 4 for every edge e in E(G), then for every e in E(G) there exists an inclusion-minimal H_e subseteq G - e with chi_DP(H_e) >= 4; this is the edge-avoidable DP-core associated to e.
minimal_dp_non3_is_dp4_critical: Any inclusion-minimal H with chi_DP(H) >= 4 is DP-4-critical: every edge f of H satisfies chi_DP(H - f) = 3 (Bernshteyn 2017 DP-edge-deletion rigidity).

bkp_dp4_critical_density: For every DP-4-critical graph H != K_4, the edge-vertex inequality 2 |E(H)| >= (40/13) |V(H)| holds; under the sharper non-Dirac DP-Brooks-type branch, 2 |E(H)| > 3 |V(H)| + 1 (Bernshteyn-Kostochka-Pron 2017 SIAM J. Discrete Math. Theorem 1.1).

k4_no_critical_edge_global_density: If G is 4-vertex-critical, every edge non-critical, and additionally chi_DP(G - v) <= 3 for all v in V(G) (DP-vertex-criticality), then 2 |E(G)| >= (40/13) |V(G)| + 2; an audit-flagged conditional restriction whose first hypothesis is the open DP-vertex-criticality strengthening of ordinary 4-vertex-criticality.
honest_disclaimer: fit_score 0.30. The DP-coloring transplant is technique-disjoint from (a) Hadwiger / minor (W6/yolo6_hadwiger), (b) discharging (W2 discharging), and (c) spectral Hoffman-Nikiforov-Tait-Wong (W2 spectral) covered earlier in this run. WHAT THIS CAN DELIVER: a Lean formalisation of the chi <= chi_DP inequality, the inclusion-minimal DP-core extraction lemma, and the Bernshteyn-Kostochka-Pron density bound at k = 4 — three reusable infrastructure pieces in FormalConjecturesForMathlib. The bridge lemma (edge-avoidable DP-core, dense DP-4-critical density restriction) is a clean structural consequence and is novel in the Dirac k = 4 literature insofar as no published paper combines DP-coloring rigidity with the no-critical-edge condition. WHAT THIS CANNOT DELIVER: a resolution of Erdős 944. The dangerous gap is exactly the DP-vertex-critical strengthening (does ordinary 4-vertex-criticality imply chi_DP(G - v) <= 3 for all v?); ordinary list-vertex-criticality does not force list-DP-vertex-criticality in general. Without this strengthening, the H_e cores need not be spanning and we obtain only a per-edge restriction. The Codex consultation (May 30 2026, GPT-5.5, xhigh reasoning) explicitly flagged this as the unresolved structural bridge. Honest fit: technique-disjoint, useful infrastructure, NOT a closure path. Aristotle is asked either (a) to attempt the DP-vertex-critical strengthening proof or (b) to construct a DP-critical witness directly via the Hajos-calculus reachable closure of K_4 under DP-Hajos-join. Gemini and Grok consultations were attempted but failed (Gemini quota-exhausted 15h+; Grok CLI raw-mode crash). The Codex web-search trail confirmed no published paper specifically applies DP-coloring to Dirac's k = 4 case; this submission is genuinely exploratory and technique-disjoint from prior W1/W2/W6 Erdős-944 attempts.
